mapping.a_element: quantum system coupled to a reservoir producing dissipation (atom-cavity mode with cavity losses)
mapping.b_element: quantum system coupled to a bath producing dissipation (generic dissipative quantum system)

mapping.a_element: microscopically derived dissipator in the master equation
mapping.b_element: quantum fluctuation-dissipation relation / Langevin friction-plus-noise term

brief_justification: Both concern the microscopic origin of dissipation for an open quantum system coupled to a reservoir, but the transferable machinery differs: paper A constructively derives a Lindblad-type master-equation dissipator for the Jaynes-Cummings model beyond the RWA, whereas paper B is a foundational critique of whether the strong (Langevin-equation) form of the quantum fluctuation-dissipation theorem can be validly derived. Different formalisms (master equation/dissipator vs. Langevin equation/fluctuation-dissipation) and different aims (deriving specific dynamics vs. assessing derivability) mean no role-for-role mapping of a common governing process holds.
